candidates.title: The Absorption Tax: How Feature Hierarchy Structure Governs SAE Failure Modes Across Semantic Domains
candidates.summary: Iteration 11 focus: fix 11-iteration data integrity debt + restructure contributions to match evidence. CONTRIBUTIONS RESTRUCTURED: (1) Probe degradation methodology (R^2=0.777, rho=-1.0) as methodological advance, (2) Universal causal mechanism via activation patching (d=0.75-1.50) as empirical advance, (3) Cross-domain characterization with confound decomposition as application context. KEY FIXES: validate_integration.py (11-iter systemic), per-token aggregation unified, Table 3 CI inversion, 4.1x->2.7x quality-gated, cross-domain patching spot-check. NEGATIVE RESULTS: GAS (rho=0.12), CMI (rho=0.044), Absorption Tax (rho=0.08).
hypotheses: H1: Cross-domain absorption rates differ (SUPPORTED: within-RAVEL p=7.4e-66, vs first-letter city-language p_Bonf=0.003)
H2': Semantic >= Syntactic at L24 (PARTIALLY SUPPORTED: city-country/city-continent yes, city-language no)
H3: Hedging decomposition varies by hierarchy (SUPPORTED: strict 0-22.6% vs loose 92.6%)
H4: GAS detector (REFUTED: rho=0.12)
H5: Absorption tax quantitative (NOT SUPPORTED: rho=0.08)
H6: Architecture ranking consistent (INCONCLUSIVE: underpowered, JumpReLU consistently best)
H7: Causal absorption universal (SUPPORTED: d=1.33/1.50/0.75, all p<0.001)
H8: Decoder direction magnitude (SUPPORTED reframed: 3.98/6.16 nats vs 0.12 control)
H9: Rate-distortion predictors (UNTESTED: target rho>0.5)
H10: Probe degradation (SUPPORTED: R^2=0.777, rho=-1.0, p=0.009)
candidates.pilot_focus: COMPLETED extensive across 10 iterations. This iteration: zero-GPU data integrity fixes + verification experiments (2 GPU-hours) + comprehensive writing revision.
candidates.key_novelty: Probe degradation methodology (8/10). Universal causal mechanism (8/10). Cross-domain characterization (7/10). Tightened hedging (7/10). Negative results (6/10).
candidates.crowded_risks: No competing cross-domain work found as of April 2026. SAEBench contains both ingredients but nobody has combined them. Field active on architectural mitigations, not characterization.
candidates.compute_estimate: ~2-4 GPU-hours new experiments + ~13 hours wall-clock total (mostly zero-GPU fixes and writing).
candidates.falsification_conditions: Cross-domain patching spot-check fails (sign reversal unreliable) AND per-token recomputation changes significance AND probe degradation for city-continent contradicts extrapolation. All three needed to invalidate core claims.
candidates.blocking_dependency: Phase 0 (validate_integration.py + aggregation fix + CI fix) blocks all paper revision. Phase 1.1 (spot-check) blocks causal mechanism claim.
candidates.venue_target: Primary: NeurIPS 2026 / ICLR 2027. Fallback: EMNLP 2026 / NeurIPS MI Workshop.
key_changes_from_prior_round: Contributions restructured: probe degradation methodology promoted to #1, universal causal mechanism to #2, cross-domain characterization to #3 (CRIT005)
validate_integration.py BLOCKING as Phase 0.1 -- 11-iteration systemic fix (MAJ008)
Per-token aggregation chosen as canonical -- eliminates 27.1% vs 21.6% contradiction (CRIT004)
Table 3 CI inversion fix -- recompute all CIs with matched aggregation (CRIT001)
First-letter patching re-run at L24 for Figure 4 consistency (MAJ002)
City-country patching exclusion documented or executed (MAJ001)
Abstract density reduced to 5-7 key numbers (MIN002)
Probe degradation section reframed: linear primary, monotonicity over R^2 (CRIT003, MAJ006)

candidates.title: Controlled Dictionary Experiment: Separating Encoder from Dictionary in Absorption Causation
candidates.summary: Hold decoder dictionary constant, vary encoder (feedforward vs. OMP vs. 2-pass). Tests whether absorption is encoder or dictionary failure. Strengthened by FULL-mode activation patching (d=0.75-1.50) confirming encoder-level competitive exclusion.
hypotheses: If amortization gap dominates: OMP absorption < 50% of feedforward at matched L0
If sparsity landscape dominates: absorption similar across encoding methods
2-pass shows intermediate absorption
candidates.pilot_focus: Feedforward vs. OMP comparison on 1000 tokens, Gemma Scope L12-16k. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 8/10. First controlled dictionary experiment. No prior work isolates encoder vs. dictionary.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if spot-check (Phase 1.1) reveals patching data is unreliable, undermining Contribution #2.

candidates.title: Feature Absorption as Competitive Exclusion: Phase Transitions and Limiting Similarity
candidates.summary: Formalize absorption through ecological Lotka-Volterra competition and phase-transition theory. Zero additional GPU cost. Provides theoretical 'why' to complement empirical 'what.'
hypotheses: Phase transition: absorption probability sigmoid with transition width < 0.1
Hierarchy-dependent theta*: first-letter < city-language < city-continent
Lotka-Volterra scaling: theta* ~ sqrt(1/W) * (L0)^(-1/2)
candidates.pilot_focus: Plot absorption probability vs. decoder cosine similarity from existing data. 15 min CPU.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. Zero prior work on competitive exclusion or phase transitions in SAE absorption.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate as supplementary analysis when Phase 0-2 writing is complete.

candidates.title: Absorption-Aware Post-Hoc Feature Correction
candidates.summary: Inference-time correction: re-activate absorbed parent features when child fires. First evaluation of post-hoc absorption correction.
hypotheses: Post-hoc correction recovers >= 50% of SAE-vs-probe gap on downstream tasks
OR: correction negligible because absorption permanently alters decoder geometry
candidates.pilot_focus: Apply correction to first-letter absorption. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 8/10. All existing mitigations are training-time. First inference-time approach.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if reviewers ask for prescriptive guidance.

candidates.title: Within-Hierarchy Variation: Why Europe Absorbs at 90% While Africa Absorbs at 4%
candidates.summary: Depth alternative to breadth. Per-class absorption variation within a single hierarchy. Predictable from class size, training frequency, decoder similarity.
hypotheses: Per-class absorption predictable from class size + log(frequency) + cos_sim (R^2 > 0.5)
Extreme class imbalance explains higher absorption in semantic hierarchies
candidates.pilot_focus: Per-continent absorption rates from existing data. 30 min CPU.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if reviewers demand deeper mechanistic analysis within a single hierarchy.

dropped_candidates.title: GAS as Primary Unsupervised Absorption Detector
Absorption Tax with Quantitative Predictions
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Quantitative predictions not supported: rho=0.08, ranking rho=-0.20, concordance 50%. Qualitative framework only.

dropped_candidates.title: EDA as Universal Weight-Only Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Replaced by GAS, which also failed.

dropped_candidates.title: ITAC as Primary Absorption Detection Method
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Core unsupervised hypothesis refuted by GAS failure.

dropped_candidates.title: Hierarchy-Coherent SAE Loss
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Crowded by HSAE, KronSAE. Requires SAE retraining (violates training-free constraint).
